title: Drawer
description: Drawer reveals contextual panels from a screen edge without forcing full page navigation. Use it for side tasks, inspection, editing, and workflows that should stay connected to the current page.
intro: Drawer supports overlay and inline presentation, edge positioning, structured footer actions, modal versus non-modal behavior, and focus management. It is a better fit than a dialog when users need more room or should keep page context visible beside the panel.

featureSections.title: Basic overlay drawer
featureSections.description: Use an overlay drawer when the panel should sit above the page while keeping the underlying context recognizable in the background.

featureSections.title: Overlay and inline types
featureSections.description: Overlay works for temporary task panels across the whole viewport. Inline is better when the drawer belongs to a bounded region such as a details pane or embedded workspace.

featureSections.title: Positions and sizes
featureSections.description: Choose the edge and panel size based on content shape. Side drawers suit forms and navigation, while top or bottom drawers fit summaries, pickers, or step confirmations.

featureSections.title: Closable, static, and alert behavior
featureSections.description: Most drawers should dismiss naturally, but interruptive flows may need static backdrop or alert-style behavior. Reserve those stricter modes for cases where casual dismissal would be risky.

featureSections.title: Footer actions
featureSections.description: Primary, secondary, and additional actions let the drawer act like a compact task surface. Keep the footer focused so users understand the main next step.

featureSections.title: Task panel pattern
featureSections.description: A realistic drawer often contains form controls, grouped settings, and commit actions. This pattern is more useful than a bare shell because it mirrors a real product surface.

accessibility.title: Role and modality
accessibility.body:
Overlay drawers render with `role="dialog"`; inline drawers render as `role="region"`. Modal overlays also expose `aria-modal` so assistive technologies understand that focus is constrained.

| Mode | Accessibility behavior |
| --- | --- |
| overlay modal | `role="dialog"` with constrained focus |
| overlay non-modal | dialog semantics without full interruption |
| inline | `role="region"` inside page flow |
| alert | strongest interruption; avoid for routine tasks |

accessibility.title: Accessible title
accessibility.body: When `title` is present, the drawer is linked through `aria-labelledby` to the visible heading. Give the title enough context that users immediately understand the panel purpose.

accessibility.title: Focus and dismissal
accessibility.body:
Modal overlay drawers trap focus while open. `Escape` and backdrop dismissal depend on `closable`, `backdrop`, and `modalType`; `alert` mode disables backdrop and Escape dismissal.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` | moves through interactive drawer content |
| `Escape` | closes dismissible overlay drawers |
| `Enter` / `Space` | activates focused controls inside the drawer |

apiSections.title: DrawerComponent - ui-drawer
apiSections.lead: Standalone sliding panel for contextual tasks, inspection, and editing flows. It supports overlay or inline layout, directional placement, focus handling, and a structured footer action area.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Visible heading used as the drawer title and `aria-labelledby` target when present.
Optional plain body copy shown before projected content.
Anchor edge for the drawer. Left and right are automatically mirrored in RTL layouts.
Controls whether clicking the overlay backdrop can dismiss the drawer when dismissal is allowed.
Controls whether the close button renders and whether Escape-based close is allowed.
Controls panel width or height depending on the active position.
Overlay renders above the viewport; inline stays within the page layout.
Controls whether the body region scrolls when content exceeds the available space.
Modal traps focus and blocks interaction, non-modal allows background interaction, alert disables backdrop and Escape dismissal.
Two-way visibility state for opening and closing the drawer.
Primary footer action, typically the main save or continue command.
Secondary footer action, usually cancel or back.
Extra footer actions rendered on the left side of the footer.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emitted after the drawer is closed by built-in close handling.
Emitted when a dynamic overlay backdrop click dismisses the drawer.
Emitted when built-in close handling updates the open state.

paragraphs: Projected content renders in the body after bodyText, so drawers can host forms, summaries, lists, or other structured layouts.
Overlay modal drawers trap focus with Angular CDK while visible. Inline drawers do not behave as full modal dialogs.

blocks.title: Related mechanisms
items: QuickAction - action model used for footer buttons.
Angular CDK A11y and bidi directionality - power focus trapping and RTL-aware positioning.
